player_notes.stephen curry: Curry is in excellent form heading into the GSW vs PHX play-in game, dropping 35 pts with 7 threes in the win over the Clippers. High confidence on points and 3PM props.
player_notes.lamelo ball: Ball avoided suspension (only fined) for tripping Adebayo; he is available for CHA vs ORL play-in game with no minutes restriction noted.
player_notes.jalen green: No direct injury or availability news; items reference Warriors' Draymond/Steph — no material prop impact for Green flagged.

game_notes.ATL vs NYK: Hawks backup C Jock Landale is out for at least the first two weeks of the playoffs with a sprained right ankle — minor frontcourt rotation shift for Atlanta.
game_notes.CHA vs ORL: LaMelo Ball (CHA) confirmed available after avoiding suspension; Desmond Bane now with Orlando, signaling a role/lineup change for the Magic that could affect Banchero/Wagner usage.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Stephen Curry (GSW) — Right knee ('runner's knee'). Missed 27 games. Returned Apr 7 on ~25 min restriction. Only 3 games since return. Still ramping for play-in. Per-minute production solid but conditioning uncertain.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news confirms Curry scored 35 points (27 in the second half) with 7 three-pointers to lead GSW past LAC 126-121 in the play-in on Apr 15-16. GSW has now advanced through the play-in and is in the playoff bracket. Curry's performance suggests he is no longer in a minutes-restriction ramp-up phase — he played a full, high-usage game in a high-stakes situation.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Curry's status: minutes restriction appears lifted or significantly relaxed based on play-in performance. He is now confirmed in the playoff bracket as GSW's 8-seed West. Remove 'conditioning uncertain' caveat and note he delivered a 35-point performance in the play-in. Update play-in results section to reflect GSW advanced (already partially reflected in context but Curry's health status needs update).

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Apr 17: ORL vs CHA (East 8-seed final) | PHX vs GSW (West 8-seed final) — listed as upcoming/pending
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news (published Apr 16) confirms GSW already defeated LAC 126-121 in the play-in on Apr 15-16. The context doc's play-in results show 'Apr 15: GSW (10) def. LAC (9) West — LAC eliminated' which is correct, but the Apr 17 West final is listed as PHX vs GSW. However, multiple headlines reference 'Dillon Brooks has the Suns one win away from the playoffs as they host the Warriors on Friday (10 p.m. ET, Prime)' — confirming PHX vs GSW is the Apr 17 final, which is consistent with the context. No direct conflict on the bracket itself, but Curry's play-in performance is confirmed and his health/ramp-up notes are now stale.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Confirm PHX vs GSW is the Apr 17 West 8-seed final (consistent with context). After that game resolves, update the OKC R1 opponent accordingly. Primary action is updating Curry's player note as flagged above.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Joel Embiid (PHI) — Emergency appendectomy (Apr 10 in Houston). No return timeline. Typical recovery ~23 days. Almost certainly out for play-in AND early R1.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News headline states 'Maxey scores 31 to lead Sixers past Magic' and 'With Joel Embiid watching from the bench' — confirms Embiid was present at the play-in game but did not play. This is consistent with the context doc's assessment that he is out. However, 'watching from the bench' suggests he has traveled with the team and is present courtside, which is a minor status update (he is ambulatory and with the team) but does not conflict with his listed OFS/out status.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Note that Embiid was on the bench (not playing) for PHI's play-in win Apr 15. He remains out but is traveling with the team. The ~23-day recovery timeline from Apr 10 appendectomy still points to earliest return around May 3 — consistent with context. No change to OFS classification needed, but add note that he is with the team.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: No mention of Jock Landale injury in the season context document.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News headline: 'Hawks backup C Landale out for beginning of series vs. Knicks' — Atlanta Hawks center Jock Landale will miss at least the first two weeks of the NBA playoffs because of a sprained right ankle.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Jock Landale (ATL) to the playoff injury landscape as out for at least the first two weeks of R1 vs NYK (sprained right ankle). As a backup center this has limited direct prop impact, but could affect ATL's frontcourt rotations against NYK's Mitchell Robinson and Karl-Anthony Towns. Note in the NYK vs ATL series diary.
